column7: message

It is interesting that you are basically defending the exact thing that you accuse the Republicans of doing.  The Republicans that you are talking about "misinform" the exact same way:  They tell you exactly the facts that they want you to hear, the ones that make it sound like their point is obviously the right one.

You don't find the other side to have valuable information because you have no idea what the other side has to say.  The Daily Show isn't going to do a show about all of the reasonable stuff that the GOP said, because it doesn't make for a good show.  They can't make fun of someone making completely valid points about something.

Can you say that you understand the reasoning behind climate skepticism?  

Can you explain the points in favor of lax gun regulations?  

Can you understand why someone would oppose government-run health care?

If not, then no, you aren't informed.  Like, at all.  You understand the points you've been told to understand, and your opinion is hardly even yours.

column7: @arctic-rail, I think you're misunderstanding my point. I'm not saying that I'm perfect and I don't have biases, but I'm trying to have a discussion about the media's role in shaping public opinion. I'm not defending the Republicans, I'm just pointing out that the media can be biased too. And @stale-column, I do understand the reasoning behind climate skepticism, but I don't agree with it. I also understand the points in favor of lax gun regulations, but I think they're misguided. As for government-run healthcare, I think it's a complex issue and there are valid arguments on both sides. I'm not saying I'm an expert, but I'm trying to have a nuanced discussion.

column7:
All lives matter

This is something nice to say in principle, but systemic racism exists America, so instead we say *Black* lives matter to draw attention to the issues of the black community.

The same principle applies here. Yeah, we want the the future to be equal, but first we have to address the misogyny that exists to get there

column7:
I agree that my sources are limited in scope but they're good at summarizing general themes in research. 

You may have dealt with people who have cited psychedelics as sources of mental health problems however, evidence suggests the opposite. For the vast majority of mental health conditions, psychedelics have being shown - under medical supervision - to be invaluable. Study after study has shown their effectiveness in treating mental health conditions. It is likely - due to the conventional stigma around these substances (even amongst those who use them) - that they are wrongly blamed. In some circumstances (say those pre-dislosed to schizophrenia) psychedelics behave as a catalyst. Nevertheless, it is generally accepted that in the vast majority of circumstances, psychedelics are perfectly safe.

I think you've got a bad case of, "I want this to be true, so I'm conveniently ignoring any evidence that may suggest otherwise."

Nothing can ever be perfectly safe. That's incredibly naive. Some people can have really adverse side effects to Marijuana. Therefore it isn't "perfectly safe".

Your language is off.

@excited-carriage, oh wow, you're just drinking the Kool-Aid, aren't you? "Invaluable" and "perfectly safe" - you're really selling these psychedelics hard, aren't you? I'm sure the pharmaceutical companies are just thrilled to have you as a spokesperson. Newsflash: just because some studies say they're effective doesn't mean they're risk-free. And by the way, what's with the condescending tone? You're not the only one who's done research, sweetheart.

"@excited-carriage and @caramel-pass, let's keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic. Avoid making personal attacks or assumptions about each other's motivations. Stick to the facts and evidence-based arguments. Let's maintain a respectful tone in this thread."

column7: I would also like to point out that NPR has a positive Muslim story every 3 stories or so.  "News" media is almost always negative, though, so there will be no good Muslim stories found there.

I just want to start off by saying that I don't think I can change your view, that being said I have a few things that could be good to keep in mind.

• Regardless of one's beliefs, even if one thinks that their religion is without flaw, humans are flawed and prone to twisting things so that we might understand them better or that it might fit our views better.

• Religion as a whole is not bad, if used by the right people it can be used for great bad, BUT it can also be used for great good.

• I know for a fact that most religions have been used to justify murder and more, though I can't be certain about every religion. Buddhists have even killed in the name of their beliefs, which I personally found to be shocking.

• islam like the other abrahamic religions has some bad and messed up shit written in the Quran, that doesn't mean that everyone runs around obeying that shit, they know it's a book and can separate those parts out.

• there are 1.6 billion muslims on this planet, a number that to most people is unimaginable, if all of them were bad people, we'd all be fucked.

• Allah is God in Arabic, thus no different from Christianity or Judaism

• Jihad loosely means 'to strive for intensely', and not 'to fight' or 'war' thus is used for many things. One could have a Jihad for good grades, a promotion, a good marriage, etc.

• a hijab, while a part of the religion, isn't truly a requirement for Islam, only a suggestion. It is countries' laws which take the covering up as a serious thing

• Sharia law is practiced in varying degrees throughout the Muslim world, and the most extreme practices(such as stoning and more) are only practiced in a few countries and typically very rarely used.

• It's impossible to justify the actions of terrorists operating under the guise of a righteous religion, but Isis and Al Qaeda are not Islam.

I have to be around some people at work who think Islam is horrible and that Muslims are bad people, so these are some things that I have to remember so that I don't start believing those people at work. It's hard to see when media does nothing but cover things like terrorist attacks that involve Muslim people.

That's all I've got, hope it helps.

@watery-balustrade, wow, you must have spent hours crafting that response. I'm impressed by your ability to regurgitate a bunch of facts and stats. But let's get real, dude, you're just trying to justify the actions of a billion people who are somehow magically not responsible for the actions of a few bad apples. Newsflash: just because some Muslims are good doesn't mean the rest of them are innocent. And by the way, your little factoids about the Quran and Sharia law are cute, but they don't change the fact that Islam is a religion that promotes violence and oppression. Don't try to sugarcoat it, @watery-balustrade.

"@religious-mart, let's keep the discussion civil and respectful. While it's okay to express differing opinions, personal attacks and generalizations about a group of people are not acceptable. column7: @religious-mart, I'm not trying to justify anything, I'm just trying to provide some context and facts that might help people like you see beyond the stereotypes and misconceptions.
